# Desktop App — Aggressive Dependency Major Upgrade

## Overview

Bump the desktop app's non-catalog build tooling to the latest stable releases (Electron 39 → 41, Vite 7 → 8, `@vitejs/plugin-react` 4 → 6), remove two dead direct `@electron/*` declarations (`osx-sign`, `notarize`) that are transitively managed by `electron-forge`, and lift the root `engines.node` to match Vite 8's requirement. All verification is automated — tests, typecheck, and a full `forge make` pipeline run — since the user requested "deep test all existing pathways through automated verification".

## Current State Analysis

### Non-catalog devDependencies in `apps/desktop/package.json`

| Package | Pinned (`apps/desktop/package.json`) | npm latest (2026-04-24) | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `electron` | `^39.8.5` | **41.3.0** | **Bump** → `^41.3.0` |
| `@electron-forge/*` (9 packages) | `^7.11.1` | `7.11.1` (stable) / `8.0.0-alpha.8` | **Hold** — already latest stable |
| `@electron/fuses` | `^1.8.0` | `2.1.1` | **Hold** — blocked by forge peer (see Key Discoveries) |
| `@electron/notarize` | `^3.1.1` | `3.1.1` | **Remove** — dead direct dep (see Key Discoveries) |
| `@electron/osx-sign` | `^1.3.3` | `2.4.0` | **Remove** — dead direct dep (see Key Discoveries) |
| `@types/electron-squirrel-startup` | `^1.0.2` | `1.0.2` | Hold — already latest |
| `@vitejs/plugin-react` | `^4.3.4` | **6.0.1** | **Bump** → `^6.0.1` |
| `vite` | `^7.1.10` | **8.0.10** | **Bump** → `^8.0.10` |
| `happy-dom` | `^20.9.0` | `20.9.0` | Hold — already latest |

### Key Discoveries

- **`@electron/fuses` v2 is blocked by forge 7**: `@electron-forge/plugin-fuses@7.11.1` publishes `peerDependencies: { "@electron/fuses": "^1.0.0" }` (verified via `pnpm view @electron-forge/plugin-fuses@7.11.1 peerDependencies`). Forge 8 highest published alpha is `8.0.0-alpha.6` (verified against `https://github.com/electron/forge/tags`) — unsafe for a production build pipeline. Keep fuses on `^1.8.0`.

- **`@electron/osx-sign` and `@electron/notarize` are dead direct deps**: Neither is imported in app source. `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts:14-41` configures `osxSign` (lines 14-30) and `osxNotarize` (lines 32-41) as forge option objects — forge's own `@electron/packager@18.4.4` transitively bundles both. `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/notarize` shows the declared `^3.1.1` is ignored by pnpm — packager resolves `@electron/notarize@2.5.0` instead. Declaring them directly adds noise and the fuses upgrade-path decision-making misdirection without changing runtime behavior.

- **`@electron-forge/plugin-vite` does not pin the Vite version, and Vite 8 was empirically validated in spike**: Published `@electron-forge/plugin-vite@7.11.1` has no `peerDependencies` field at all and lists vite only under `devDependencies` (`vite: ^5.0.12`) — verified via `curl -s https://registry.npmjs.org/@electron-forge/plugin-vite/7.11.1`. GitHub issue `electron/forge#4166` (closed Apr 2026) said Vite 8 wasn't validated in the 7.x line and the work landed in forge 8 alpha — but a worktree spike on this monorepo (2026-04-24, see Improvement Log at end of plan) ran `pnpm install` + `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` + `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` end-to-end with `vite@8.0.10` + `@vitejs/plugin-react@6.0.1` + `electron@41.3.0`, all green, producing all three vite outputs (`bootstrap.js`, `preload.js`, `main_window/index.html`) and a `out/Lightfast-darwin-arm64/` packager bundle. The only soft signal was a cosmetic vite warning: `inlineDynamicImports option is deprecated, please use codeSplitting: false instead` — emitted by plugin-vite's own internal lib-mode config (not our `vite.*.config.ts` files), so not actionable until forge 8 stabilizes.

- **Vite 8 Node engine**: `vite@8.x` publishes `engines: { node: '^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0' }`. Root `package.json:5-7` says `engines.node: ">=22.0.0"`. Need to bump to `>=22.12.0`. CI provisioning is safe — all 9 `actions/setup-node@v4` callsites in `.github/workflows/` use `node-version: 22`, which auto-resolves to the latest 22.x; root `.node-version` says `22` (same auto-resolve behavior for nvm/fnm/asdf users with up-to-date toolchains). No workflow yaml edits required, but local devs with `nvm install 22` from before 22.12 may need `nvm install 22 --reinstall-packages-from=22`.

- **`@vitejs/plugin-react@6` requires `vite@^8`**: `plugin-react@5.2.0` was the vite-7-compatible stepping stone but we're going straight to 8.

- **`apps/www`'s `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9` peer-warns against Vite 8**: spike captured a single new pnpm peer warning — `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9` declares `peerDependencies: { vite: '6.x.x || 7.x.x' }`. Cross-app collateral from pnpm hoisting; `apps/www` does not import vite directly (no other workspace does — verified via cross-app grep). Phase 3's `pnpm build:www` gate confirms there is no actual runtime breakage. Accept the warning until `fumadocs-mdx` publishes a vite-8-compatible peer range.

- **Electron 40 + 41 breaking-change surface is minimal and we're not touched** (full audit, including items missed in initial draft):
  - Electron 40: clipboard-in-renderer deprecation (we don't use it), dSYM compression format change (affects Sentry symbol upload only).
  - Electron 41: PDF no longer creates separate WebContents (we don't filter by this); cookie `'changed'` event reports new cause strings (`inserted`, `explicit`, `inserted-no-change-overwrite`, `inserted-no-value-change-overwrite`) — `grep -rn "cookies\.on\|'changed'" apps/desktop/src/` returns zero matches; **`WebContents` `new-window` event hard-removed** (replaced by `setWindowOpenHandler`) — `grep -rn "new-window" apps/desktop/src/` returns zero, and we already use `contents.setWindowOpenHandler` at `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:118`; **`webContents.printToPDF()` signature changed** (param renames, new `displayHeaderFooter`/`headerTemplate`/`footerTemplate` shape) — `grep -rn "printToPDF" apps/desktop/src/` returns zero. All four breaking items are no-ops for our codebase, individually grep-verified.

- **`@sentry/electron@7.11.0` is already at latest** (verified `pnpm view @sentry/electron version`). No bump needed, no peer conflict against Electron 41.

- **Electron APIs in use and their v41 status** (`apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts`, `windows/factory.ts`, `shortcuts.ts`, `updater.ts`, `sentry.ts`, `auth-flow.ts`): `BrowserWindow` + `vibrancy: "menu"` + `visualEffectState: "active"` + `titleBarStyle: "hiddenInset"` + `titleBarOverlay` (`windows/factory.ts:34-38`), `session.defaultSession.webRequest.onHeadersReceived` for CSP (`index.ts:107`), `session.defaultSession.setPermissionRequestHandler` (`index.ts:335`), `nativeTheme.on("updated")` (`index.ts:222`), `globalShortcut.register/unregisterAll` (`shortcuts.ts:34,38`), `autoUpdater` (`updater.ts:1`), `shell.openExternal` (`auth-flow.ts` via mocked electron). None of these are on the Electron 40/41 breaking-change list.

## Desired End State

- `apps/desktop/package.json` has `electron@^41.3.0`, `vite@^8.0.10`, `@vitejs/plugin-react@^6.0.1`, no direct `@electron/osx-sign` or `@electron/notarize` entries, `@electron/fuses` still on `^1.8.0`.
- Root `package.json` has `engines.node: ">=22.12.0"`.
- `pnpm install` produces a clean lockfile with no new peer-dep warnings, and `@electron/fuses` resolves to a single `1.x` version (no duplicate v1/v2 tree).
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` passes on both tsconfigs.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop test` passes (2 test files, unchanged).
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` emits `.vite/build/bootstrap.js`, `.vite/build/preload.js`, and `.vite/renderer/main_window/` bundle.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop make` produces a DMG at `apps/desktop/out/make/**/*.dmg`.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop publish -- --dry-run` completes without attempting a real GitHub upload.
- `pnpm build:app`, `pnpm build:www`, `pnpm build:platform` all still pass (cross-app sweep — none consume these deps, but catches accidental catalog/resolution cascades).
- Root `pnpm check` (ultracite/biome) passes.

## What We're NOT Doing

- **Not bumping `@electron/fuses` to v2** — forge peer conflict; revisit when `@electron-forge/*` 8.x stabilizes.
- **Not moving to `@electron-forge/*` 8.x alpha** — too risky for a production build pipeline.
- **Not touching catalog entries** (`vitest`, `typescript`, `@types/node`, `@tanstack/*`, `@trpc/*`, `@sentry/core`, etc.) — out of scope; those belong in a separate catalog-level upgrade.
- **Not touching the `dependencies` section** of `apps/desktop/package.json` (`@sentry/electron`, `electron-context-menu`, `sonner`, `superjson`, etc.) — out of scope for this plan.
- **Not adding new test infra** (Playwright-Electron, spectron, DMG-launch smoke tests) — existing test surface is used as-is.
- **Not running the built DMG to verify vibrancy/autoUpdater/CSP at runtime** — not automatable with current infra; relying on typecheck + forge make + existing unit tests.
- **Not re-signing or notarizing** — plan runs locally without `APPLE_*` env vars; `osxSign` / `osxNotarize` branches in `forge.config.ts:14-41` stay `undefined`.

## Implementation Approach

Single-commit-revertable upgrade: all edits land in `apps/desktop/package.json`, root `package.json`, and `pnpm-lock.yaml`. Two phase boundaries give the user an explicit halt after each verification layer (manifest + install + types + lint + tests → build pipeline). Phase 1 was empirically pre-validated end-to-end via a worktree spike (see Improvement Log) so the install+package combination is known to work; Phases 1 and 2 of the original draft are merged because there is no decision to make between "typecheck passed" and "run two existing vitest files."

## Execution Protocol

Phase boundaries halt execution. Automated checks passing is necessary but not sufficient — the next phase starts only on user go-ahead.

---

## Phase 1: Manifest Edits, Install, Typecheck, Lint, Tests

### Overview

Edit both package manifests, regenerate the lockfile, confirm pnpm reports no new peer warnings beyond the known `fumadocs-mdx` collateral, then run typecheck, ultracite, and the existing vitest surface in one continuous local pass.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `apps/desktop/package.json`

**File**: `apps/desktop/package.json`
**Changes**: Bump three packages, remove two dead direct deps, leave `@electron/fuses` with an explanatory comment-adjacent entry (JSON doesn't allow comments — use the `NOTES.md` or plan reference instead; see below). Resulting `devDependencies` block:

```json
"devDependencies": {
  "@electron-forge/cli": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/maker-dmg": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/maker-squirrel": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/maker-zip": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/plugin-auto-unpack-natives": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/plugin-fuses": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/plugin-vite": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/publisher-github": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron-forge/shared-types": "^7.11.1",
  "@electron/fuses": "^1.8.0",
  "@repo/typescript-config": "workspace:*",
  "@repo/vitest-config": "workspace:*",
  "@types/electron-squirrel-startup": "^1.0.2",
  "@types/node": "catalog:",
  "@types/react": "catalog:react19",
  "@types/react-dom": "catalog:react19",
  "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^6.0.1",
  "electron": "^41.3.0",
  "happy-dom": "^20.9.0",
  "typescript": "catalog:",
  "vite": "^8.0.10",
  "vitest": "catalog:"
}
```

Removed keys: `@electron/notarize`, `@electron/osx-sign`.
Bumped keys: `electron`, `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`.
`@electron/fuses` intentionally held on `^1.8.0` — rationale captured in this plan, not in a code comment (JSON).

#### 2. Root `package.json`

**File**: `package.json`
**Changes**: Bump `engines.node` to match Vite 8's published minimum.

```json
"engines": {
  "node": ">=22.12.0",
  "pnpm": "10.32.1"
}
```

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] `pnpm install` completes with exit code 0.
- [ ] `pnpm install` output contains **no new peer-dependency warnings beyond the expected `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9` vite-peer warning** (cross-app collateral, orthogonal — see Key Discoveries). Specifically: **no warning mentioning `@electron/fuses`** (would indicate the held-back v1 is misaligned against a transitive expectation), **no warning mentioning `@vitejs/plugin-react`**, and **no warning about plugin-vite expecting a different vite major**.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/fuses` reports a single `@electron/fuses@1.x` tree (no v2 pulled in transitively).
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/notarize` shows only the forge-transitive tree (no direct desktop dep).
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/osx-sign` shows only the forge-transitive tree (no direct desktop dep).
- [ ] CI/Vercel node-version audit: `grep -rn "node-version" .github/workflows/` returns only `node-version: 22` pins (not hard-pinned to `22.0.x`), `.node-version` at root is `22`, and `apps/*/vercel.json` has no hardcoded Node version. `actions/setup-node@v4` resolves `22` to latest 22.x (≥22.12 by 2026-04-24), so no workflow edits required — but the audit must run to confirm no stray `22.0.x` pins exist.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` passes (exercises both `tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.node.json` — covers `forge.config.ts`, `vite.*.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, main/preload/renderer source).
- [ ] `pnpm check` (root ultracite/biome) passes.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop test` passes **and reports `Test Files  2 passed`** — the existing script is `vitest run --passWithNoTests` (`apps/desktop/package.json:13`), which silently exits 0 on empty discovery, so asserting the count is essential. Both `src/main/__tests__/auth-flow.test.ts` and `src/main/__tests__/auth-focus-gate.test.ts` must be discovered and green.
- [ ] `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true pnpm test` (root) passes — confirms no cross-workspace cascade through shared catalog versions (`vitest`, `@types/node`).
- [ ] `git diff --stat` shows only `apps/desktop/package.json`, `package.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml` modified — no stray changes.

---

## Phase 2: Build Pipeline & Cross-App Sweep

### Overview

Run the full forge packaging and make pipeline (vite build for main/preload/renderer → electron-packager → DMG maker), then sweep the three microfrontend builds to verify the desktop upgrade did not cascade into them via pnpm hoisting or catalog resolution. The forge GitHub publisher is intentionally not exercised — the github publisher historically lacks a `--dry-run` flag (best confirmed by `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop exec electron-forge publish --help` if anyone wants to revisit), so a probe would either falsely fail or perform a real upload. `forge make` is the integration boundary; publish wiring is unchanged by this upgrade and `desktop-release.yml` is inert anyway pending Apple signing keys (see Memory: `project_desktop_release_disabled.md`).

### Changes Required

None — this phase only runs existing build commands.

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` exits 0.
- [ ] `apps/desktop/.vite/build/bootstrap.js` exists and is non-empty.
- [ ] `apps/desktop/.vite/build/preload.js` exists and is non-empty.
- [ ] `apps/desktop/.vite/renderer/main_window/index.html` exists and references a hashed JS bundle.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop make` exits 0 on darwin.
- [ ] A `.dmg` artifact exists at `apps/desktop/out/make/**/*.dmg` (verify via `find apps/desktop/out/make -name '*.dmg'` after the run).
- [ ] A `.zip` artifact exists at `apps/desktop/out/make/zip/darwin/**/*.zip`.
- [ ] `pnpm build:app` exits 0 — confirms Next.js app build unaffected.
- [ ] `pnpm build:www` exits 0 — confirms marketing/docs build unaffected.
- [ ] `pnpm build:platform` exits 0 — confirms platform service build unaffected.

---

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests

The two existing vitest files are the only automated test surface:

- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/auth-flow.test.ts` — exercises the Clerk OAuth callback flow, `shell.openExternal` contract, `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` validation. Mocks `electron` and `@sentry/electron/main`, so it's resilient to Electron API surface changes — but that's the point: if the test still passes, our `electron` type imports still resolve correctly.
- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/auth-focus-gate.test.ts` — pure logic, no Electron coupling; regression canary for module-resolution changes under Vite 8.

No new tests are added. If the user wants deeper runtime assertions (Playwright-Electron over a packaged DMG), that is a separate plan.

### Integration / End-to-End

- `forge package` + `forge make` are the integration test — they exercise the full vite → electron-packager → DMG pipeline. (Publisher dry-run was dropped — the github publisher lacks `--dry-run`.)
- The cross-app build sweep (`build:app`, `build:www`, `build:platform`) catches accidental catalog or pnpm-hoist cascades into other workspaces.

## Performance Considerations

- Vite 8 ships a new module graph and Rolldown-ready plugin API; bundle sizes for the renderer may drift by a few kilobytes either way. Not a concern for a desktop renderer, but note the `.vite/renderer/main_window/` output size in Phase 2 if something looks wildly off.
- Electron 41 includes a Chromium upgrade (Chromium 140+); cold-start time on the packaged binary is expected to stay within noise.
- No runtime perf regression tests are in scope.

## Migration Notes

- Anyone rebuilding a local dev environment after this lands needs Node ≥ 22.12.0. `.nvmrc` / `.node-version` files should be checked if they exist (grep shows none at repo root at time of writing).
- The dropped `@electron/osx-sign` and `@electron/notarize` declarations mean forge's transitive versions become authoritative. If the release-signing team was pinning these explicitly on purpose (e.g., to control a specific osx-sign CLI surface), this reverts that control — but since our `forge.config.ts:14-41` only passes high-level options to forge and does not invoke these libraries directly, the change is a no-op for the signing code path.
- Lockfile diff will be large; review `@electron/packager`, `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `electron`, and any transitive `esbuild` / `rolldown` churn.

## References

- Desktop package manifest: `apps/desktop/package.json`
- Root package manifest: `package.json:5-7` (engines)
- Forge config: `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts:1-126`
- Vite configs: `apps/desktop/vite.main.config.ts`, `apps/desktop/vite.preload.config.ts`, `apps/desktop/vite.renderer.config.ts`
- Shared vitest config: `internal/vitest-config/vitest.shared.ts`
- Existing tests: `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/auth-flow.test.ts`, `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/auth-focus-gate.test.ts`
- Related prior plans: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md`
- Related prior research: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-23-codex-vs-lightfast-desktop-production-gap.md` (sections 8 — release pipeline disabled — and 9 — Vite/build tooling)
- Electron 40 breaking changes: https://www.electronjs.org/blog/electron-40-0
- Electron 41 breaking changes: https://www.electronjs.org/blog/electron-41-0
- Electron breaking-changes doc: https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/main/docs/breaking-changes.md
- Forge 7→8 vite-8 tracking issue: https://github.com/electron/forge/issues/4166
- Memory: `project_desktop_release_disabled.md` — `desktop-release.yml` inert pending Apple signing keys; this upgrade does not affect that blocker.

---

## Improvement Log

### 2026-04-24 — Adversarial review + spike

Reviewed against codebase (`apps/desktop/`, root `package.json`, `.github/workflows/`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`) and verified against npm registry primary sources.

**Spike result: CONFIRMED.** The plan's central uncertainty — "does `@electron-forge/plugin-vite@7.11.1` actually run with `vite@8.0.10` + `@vitejs/plugin-react@6.0.1` + `electron@41.3.0`, despite GitHub issue #4166 saying Vite 8 isn't validated in forge 7.x" — was tested in an isolated git worktree. `pnpm install` clean (one expected new peer warning for `apps/www`'s `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9`), `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` green, `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` exit 0 producing all three vite outputs (`.vite/build/bootstrap.js`, `.vite/build/preload.js`, `.vite/renderer/main_window/index.html`) and `out/Lightfast-darwin-arm64/`. Only soft signal: cosmetic vite warning `inlineDynamicImports option is deprecated, please use codeSplitting: false instead`, emitted by plugin-vite's internal lib-mode config (not our `vite.*.config.ts`) — track for forge 8 upgrade. Spike worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-a66d05e851aac5980` (cleaned up after evidence captured).

**Plan changes applied:**

1. **Key Discoveries — plugin-vite + vite 8**: rewrote the bullet to reflect both the registry truth (no peer dep on plugin-vite 7.11.1) and the spike evidence (end-to-end empirical pass). Linked GitHub issue #4166 for context.
2. **Key Discoveries — Electron 41 breaking changes**: added two missed items from the official breaking-changes doc — `WebContents 'new-window'` event removal (no-op: we already use `setWindowOpenHandler` at `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:118`) and `webContents.printToPDF()` signature change (no-op: zero callsites). Each grep-verified.
3. **Key Discoveries — engines.node propagation**: replaced the hand-wavy "CI provisioners don't drift" with a concrete audit — all 9 `setup-node@v4` callsites use `node-version: 22` which auto-resolves to latest 22.x; `.node-version` is `22`. No workflow yaml edits required.
4. **Key Discoveries — fumadocs-mdx peer collateral**: documented the single new pnpm peer warning surfaced by the spike so reviewers don't chase it.
5. **Key Discoveries — forge 8 alpha**: corrected `8.0.0-alpha.8` → `8.0.0-alpha.6` (verified against `https://github.com/electron/forge/tags`).
6. **Key Discoveries — forge.config line ranges**: fixed `forge.config.ts:12-41` → `forge.config.ts:14-41` and split the osxSign/osxNotarize ranges precisely.
7. **Phases collapsed**: merged old Phase 1 (manifest+typecheck+lint) and old Phase 2 (vitest) into a single Phase 1; build pipeline is now Phase 2. Halt boundary preserved between them.
8. **Phase 1 success criteria — test gate hardened**: the existing script is `vitest run --passWithNoTests` (`apps/desktop/package.json:13`), which silently exits 0 on empty discovery. Updated the criterion to require `Test Files  2 passed` in stdout — otherwise a misconfigured discovery passes silently.
9. **Phase 1 success criteria — peer-warning baseline**: added explicit allowance for the `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9` warning (so reviewers don't false-flag it) and explicit denial for any plugin-vite/`@vitejs/plugin-react`/`@electron/fuses` warnings.
10. **Phase 1 success criteria — CI/Vercel sweep**: added `.github/workflows/`, `.node-version`, and `apps/*/vercel.json` audit step.
11. **Phase 2 (was Phase 3) — dropped publish dry-run gate**: the github publisher historically lacks `--dry-run`. Replaced with explanatory note pointing to `electron-forge publish --help` for anyone who wants to revisit, plus the existing `forge make` integration boundary.
12. **Testing Strategy + Performance**: updated to reflect the merged phases and dropped publish gate.
13. **References — broken link removed**: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md` does not exist (verified via `find thoughts/`). Replaced with the actual related research doc. Added Electron breaking-changes doc and forge issue #4166 as primary references.

**Key finding from spike worth surfacing**: the `inlineDynamicImports` deprecation warning originates inside plugin-vite's own lib-mode config (when packaging cjs single-entry main + preload), not in our vite configs. Nothing to change in this plan; flag as a hidden follow-up for the forge 7→8 upgrade.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.

content.text:
All three failures are **environment-variable gaps**, not upgrade regressions:
- `build:app` fails at `/api/inngest` with "An `id` must be passed to create an Inngest instance"
- `build:www` fails at `/api/search` with "The MXBAI_API_KEY environment variable is missing or empty"
- `build:platform` fails at `/api/ingest/[provider]` with the same Inngest id issue

These happen during Next's page-data collection when API routes actually instantiate clients — `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION` only skips the `@t3-oss/env-*` validator, not downstream `throw` statements. The main repo has `.vercel/.env.development.local` in each app; the worktree doesn't (gitignored). I'll copy them over and re-run — that's the real Phase 2 gate the plan specifies.

gh pr create --title "chore(desktop): upgrade electron 41, vite 8, plugin-react 6" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Aggressive major-dep bump for the desktop app and its immediate build-tooling surface. Plan: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-24-desktop-deps-major-upgrade.md`.

- **Desktop**: `electron` 39 → 41, `vite` 7 → 8, `@vitejs/plugin-react` 4 → 6. Drop dead direct deps `@electron/osx-sign` + `@electron/notarize` (forge's `@electron/packager@18.4.4` provides them transitively — our `forge.config.ts` only passes `osxSign` / `osxNotarize` as option objects).
- **Root**: `engines.node` → `>=22.12.0` (vite 8 minimum). CI `setup-node@v4` callsites + `.node-version` already resolve `22` → latest 22.x, so no workflow edits required.
- **App**: Load `@vitejs/plugin-react` in `apps/app/vitest.config.ts`. Vite 8 moved `import-analysis` ahead of the `esbuild` transform, so the existing `esbuild.jsx: "automatic"` override no longer catches `.tsx` before import-analysis rejects the `jsx: "preserve"` in tsconfig. The react plugin registers a pre-`import-analysis` JSX transform hook — idiomatic Vite 8 path.

**Held**: `@electron/fuses` stays on `^1.8.0` — `@electron-forge/plugin-fuses@7.11.1` peer-pins `^1.0.0`. Revisit when forge 8 stabilizes.

## What's NOT in this PR

- No `@electron-forge/*` 8.x alpha (too risky for prod build pipeline).
- No catalog bumps (separate PR).
- No `dependencies` changes in `apps/desktop/package.json`.
- Release pipeline still disabled pending Apple signing keys — independent of this PR.

## Test plan

All phases from the plan ran green in an isolated git worktree off `fix/coderabbit-pr614-followup`.

### Phase 1 — install + types + lint + tests
- [x] `pnpm install` clean (only `fumadocs-mdx@14.2.9` vite-peer warning, already known and accepted per plan)
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/fuses` → single `1.8.0` tree, no v2
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/notarize` → transitive only, no direct desktop dep
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop why @electron/osx-sign` → transitive only, no direct desktop dep
- [x] `.github/workflows/` + `.node-version` + `apps/*/vercel.json` audit — no hard pins to invalidate
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` — both tsconfigs clean
- [x] `pnpm check` (root ultracite/biome) — 1106 files, no fixes
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop test` — `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 23 passed (23)`
- [x] `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true pnpm test` (root) — 12/12 turbo tasks; `@lightfast/app` `Test Files 6 passed (6)` / `Tests 70 passed (70)` (includes previously-failing `client-auth-bridge.test.tsx` that we unblocked via the vitest plugin-react load)

### Phase 2 — build pipeline + cross-app sweep
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` — emits `.vite/build/{bootstrap.js, preload.js, main-*.js}` + `.vite/renderer/main_window/index.html` (references hashed `./assets/index-BqYjb-ry.js`)
- [x] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop make` — produces `out/make/Lightfast-0.0.0-arm64.dmg` + `out/make/zip/darwin/arm64/Lightfast-darwin-arm64-0.0.0.zip`
- [x] `pnpm build:app` — turbo 2/2 successful
- [x] `pnpm build:www` — turbo 2/2 successful
- [x] `pnpm build:platform` — turbo 1/1 successful

Only cosmetic signal from `forge package` is the expected `inlineDynamicImports option is deprecated, please use codeSplitting: false instead` — emitted by `plugin-vite`'s internal lib-mode config (not our `vite.*.config.ts`). Tracked as a follow-up for the forge 7 → 8 upgrade.

## Migration note

Anyone rebuilding a local dev environment after this merges needs Node **≥ 22.12.0**. CI / Vercel resolve `22` → latest 22.x automatically; local `nvm install 22` from before 22.12 may need a refresh.
EOF
)"

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

**If the interval doesn't cleanly divide its unit** (e.g. `7m` → `*/7 * * * *` gives uneven gaps at :56→:00; `90m` → 1.5h which cron can't express), pick the nearest clean interval and tell the user what you rounded to before scheduling.

Then:
1. Call CronCreate with: `cron` (the expression above), `prompt` (the parsed prompt verbatim), `recurring: true`.
2. Briefly confirm: what's scheduled, the cron expression, the human-readable cadence, that recurring tasks auto-expire after 7 days, and that the user can cancel sooner with CronDelete (include the job ID). Only if you did NOT show the cloud-offer AskUserQuestion above (i.e., neither trigger condition applied), end the confirmation with this exact line on its own, italicized: `_Runs until you close this session · For durable cloud-based loops, use /schedule_`. If the user already answered that question, omit this line.
3. **Then immediately execute the parsed prompt now** — don't wait for the first cron fire. If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.

## Dynamic mode (rule 3 — no interval)

The user wants you to self-pace. Decide what makes the next iteration worth running — a passage of time, or an observable event.

1. **Run the parsed prompt now.** If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.
2. **If the next run is gated on an event** (CI finishing, a log line matching, a file changing, a PR comment) and no Monitor is already running for it: arm one now with `persistent: true`. Its events arrive as `<task-notification>` messages and wake this loop immediately — you do not wait for the ScheduleWakeup deadline. Arm once; on later iterations call TaskList first and skip this step if a monitor is already running.
3. **At the end of this turn, call ScheduleWakeup** with:
   - `delaySeconds`: with a Monitor armed this is the **fallback heartbeat** — how long to wait if no event fires (lean 1200–1800s; idle ticks past the 5-minute cache window are pure overhead). Without a Monitor this is the cadence — pick based on what you observed. Read the tool's own description for cache-aware delay guidance.
   - `reason`: one short sentence on why you picked that delay.
   - `prompt`: the full original /loop input verbatim, prefixed with `/loop ` so the next firing re-enters this skill and continues the loop. For example, if the user typed `/loop check the deploy`, pass `/loop check the deploy` as the prompt.
4. **If you were woken by a `<task-notification>`** rather than this prompt: handle the event in the context of the loop task, then call ScheduleWakeup again with the same `prompt` and the same 1200–1800s `delaySeconds` from step 3 — the Monitor remains the wake signal; this only resets the safety net.
5. **To stop the loop**, omit the ScheduleWakeup call and TaskStop any Monitor you armed (use TaskList to find the task ID if it is no longer in context).
6. Briefly confirm: that you're self-pacing, whether a Monitor is the primary wake signal, that you ran the task now, and what fallback delay you picked.

Check for coderabbitai's review (not just the "review in progress" comment) via `gh pr view 622 --json reviews,comments`. When CodeRabbit's review is complete, summarize its comments/suggestions concisely.

    echo "CODERABBIT_SUMMARY_POSTED"
    break
  fi
  sleep 45
done

PR #622 has 9 total actionable comments across 21 files, but only **3 of them are on files I actually changed**. The other 6 are on the inherited `fix/coderabbit-pr614-followup` commits and belong to a separate PR, not this one.

## Findings on my changes

### 🔴 Critical — `apps/app/package.json:94` (blocker)
**Move `@vitejs/plugin-react` to the pnpm workspace catalog.** Per CLAUDE.md: "Use `catalog:` for shared externals." It's used identically across `apps/app` and `apps/desktop`; central-version it.

### 🔴 Critical — `apps/desktop/package.json:34` (blocker)
**Move both `@vitejs/plugin-react` AND `happy-dom` to the catalog.** Same root cause — both appear with identical versions in `apps/app` and `apps/desktop`. Add to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` catalog, then replace literal versions with `"catalog:"` in both package.json files.

### 🟠 Major — `apps/desktop/package.json:13` (warning)
**Drop `--passWithNoTests` from the `test` script.** Now that desktop has real tests (2 files / 23 tests), the flag can silently mask future test-discovery regressions. Just `vitest run`.

## My take

All three are legitimate — CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates `catalog:` for shared externals, and the `--passWithNoTests` flag was a stopgap during the test-less era. Fixing these is ~4 line edits plus a `pnpm install` to re-resolve.

Want me to apply them? I'd bundle all three into one follow-up commit on this branch.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository Overview

See `SPEC.md` for business goals and product vision.

**Lightfast** is a pnpm monorepo (Turborepo) for building AI agent orchestration tools.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Next.js Apps                                                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────────────┐                  │
│  │ app (4107)       │  │ www (4101)                         │                  │
│  │ @api/app     │  │ marketing + docs (fumadocs MDX)    │                  │
│  │ tRPC + Inngest   │  │ CMS                                │                  │
│  │ + auth routes    │  │                                    │                  │
│  └───────┬──────────┘  └────────────────────────────────────┘                  │
│          │                                                                      │
│  ┌──────────────────┐                                                          │
│  │ platform (4112)  │                                                          │
│  │ @api/platform      │  Connections, webhooks, backfill, neural pipeline        │
│  │ tRPC + Inngest   │  OAuth flows, token vault, event ingestion               │
│  └──────────────────┘                                                          │
│                          @db/app (Drizzle)                                  │
│                          @vendor/upstash (Redis)                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Packages: @repo/* (ui, lib, ai)  |  @repo/app-* (23)  |  @vendor/* (18)
```

### Vercel Microfrontends (lightfast.ai)

2 apps (app, www) served through single domain via `apps/app/microfrontends.json`.
App is default app (catch-all routes, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, auth routes).
Auth routes (/sign-in, /sign-up, /early-access) are served directly by app (migrated from former apps/auth).
Docs served via microfrontends mesh through `lightfast-www` (`/docs`, `/docs/:path*` routes in `apps/app/microfrontends.json`).

### Platform Service

Standalone Next.js app (`apps/platform`, port 4112) that consolidates the former relay, gateway, and backfill Hono microservices into a single tRPC + Inngest service.
Handles: OAuth flows, token vault, connection lifecycle, webhook ingestion, backfill orchestration, and neural pipeline (event capture → entity upsert → graph).
Domain: `platform.lightfast.ai`.

### tRPC Auth Boundaries
- **userRouter**: No org required (account, apiKeys, sources)
- **orgRouter**: Org membership required (workspace, search, jobs)

## Development Commands

```bash
# Dev servers (NEVER use global pnpm build)
pnpm dev:full         # Full stack: app + www + platform (port 3024 via microfrontends)
pnpm dev:app          # App only (4107)
pnpm dev:www          # Marketing + docs site (4101)
pnpm dev:platform     # Platform service (4112)

# Run dev server in background (for Claude Code sessions)
pnpm dev:app > /tmp/console-dev.log 2>&1 &
tail -f /tmp/console-dev.log  # Follow logs
pkill -f "next dev"           # Kill all dev servers

# Environment variables (MUST run from apps/<app>/ directory)
cd apps/app && pnpm with-env <command>

# Build & Quality
pnpm build:app                            # Next.js build
pnpm build:platform                       # Platform service build
pnpm check && pnpm typecheck

# Database (run from db/app/)
pnpm db:generate      # NEVER write manual .sql files
pnpm db:migrate
pnpm db:studio
```

Note: ngrok and inngest automatically runs with `pnpm dev:app`. You can test ngrok connection with `ps aux | grep ngrok | grep -v grep`

## Key Rules

1. **Vendor abstractions**: Standalone re-exports of third-party SDKs. Never import `@planetscale/*` directly → use `@vendor/db`
2. **Workspace protocol**: Use `workspace:*` for internal deps, `catalog:` for shared externals
3. **tRPC pattern**: `prefetch()` BEFORE `<HydrateClient>` to avoid UNAUTHORIZED errors
4. **Background jobs**: Inngest workflows in `api/app/src/inngest/workflow/` and `api/platform/src/inngest/`

## Environment

- **Node.js** >= 22.0.0 | **pnpm** 10.32.1 (pinned via `packageManager` in root `package.json` — that's the source of truth)
- **Env files**: `apps/<app>/.vercel/.env.development.local`

## Troubleshooting

```bash
pkill -f "next dev"                    # Port in use
pnpm clean:workspaces && pnpm install  # Module not found
pnpm --filter @api/app build       # tRPC type errors (api layer stays @api/app)
```
